title: eatonia pensylvanica
intro: Eatonia pensylvanica, also known as American burnweed, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Eatonia pensylvanica is typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
